root_cause.text: An integer overflow occurs in the `EVP_EncryptUpdate` function when the length of previously buffered partial block data is added to a very large input length, causing the buffer capacity check to incorrectly pass and leading to a heap buffer overflow.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces the vulnerable addition `if (i + inl < bl)` with a safe subtraction `if (bl - i > inl)` to prevent the integer overflow when `inl` is very large.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: An integer overflow occurs in the `EVP_EncryptUpdate` function when the length of previously buffered partial block data is added to a very large input length, causing the buffer capacity check to incorrectly pass and leading to a heap buffer overflow.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch replaces the vulnerable addition `if (i + inl < bl)` with a safe subtraction `if (bl - i > inl)` to prevent the integer overflow when `inl` is very large.

intent_record.intent_summary: Prevent integer overflow in the block size boundary check by rearranging the arithmetic condition.
intent_record.what: Changed the arithmetic expression in the if-condition from addition (`i + inl < bl`) to subtraction (`bl - i > inl`).
intent_record.how: Prevents potential integer overflow when calculating buffer bounds, ensuring the subsequent memory copy does not exceed the buffer limits.

grouping_result.intent: Prevent integer overflow in the block size boundary check by rearranging the arithmetic condition.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the root cause by preventing the integer overflow in the block size boundary check through rearranging the arithmetic condition, which is the exact fix for the described vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This line performs an addition of 'i' (buffered data length) and 'inl' (input length) before comparing it to the block size 'bl'. If 'inl' is very large, the addition can overflow, causing the check to pass incorrectly and leading to a heap buffer overflow.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement adds the buffered data length 'i' to the input length 'inl' before comparing with the block size 'bl'. An extremely large 'inl' causes an integer overflow, bypassing the buffer capacity check and resulting in a heap buffer overflow.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 0f113f3ee4d629ef9a4a30911b22b224772085e5
Author: Matt Caswell <matt@openssl.org>
Date:   Thu Jan 22 03:40:55 2015 +0000

    Run util/openssl-format-source -v -c .
    
    Reviewed-by: Tim Hudson <tjh@openssl.org>

diff --git a/crypto/evp/evp_enc.c b/crypto/evp/evp_enc.c
index 2b62bf64d2..111351852e 100644
--- a/crypto/evp/evp_enc.c
+++ b/crypto/evp/evp_enc.c
@@ -5,21 +5,21 @@
  * This package is an SSL implementation written
  * by Eric Young (eay@cryptsoft.com).
  * The implementation
bic_trace_step.reason: The function EVP_EncryptUpdate contains the expression `if (i + inl < bl)` where `i` is `ctx->buf_len` and `inl` is the input length. Since both are signed integers, a very large `inl` can cause an integer overflow, making the sum smaller than `bl` and bypassing the buffer capacity check, which leads to a buffer overflow in the subsequent `memcpy(&(ctx->buf[i]), in, inl)`. There is no fix for this overflow present in this commit.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Handle the common case first (where input size is a multiple of block size).
Worth around 5% for encrypt. Slows down dec
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 7b6055d1afaf6eed5e422c342be33f34304430b3
Author: Ben Laurie <ben@openssl.org>
Date:   Sun Jul 8 19:42:10 2001 +0000

    Handle the common case first (where input size is a multiple of block size).
    Worth around 5% for encrypt. Slows down decrypt slightly, but I expect to
    regain that later.

diff --git a/crypto/evp/evp_enc.c b/crypto/evp/evp_enc.c
index ae1a22ebb8..d723d095c1 100644
--- a/crypto/evp/evp_enc.c
+++ b/crypto/evp/evp_enc.c
@@ -162,8 +162,25 @@ int EVP_EncryptUpdate(EVP
bic_trace_step.reason: The function contains the vulnerable construct `if (i+inl < bl)` where `i` is `ctx->buf_len` and `inl` is the input length. There are no checks to prevent `i+inl` from overflowing if `inl` is very large, which is the root cause of the vulnerability. The commit only optimizes the common case where `i == 0 && (inl&(bl-1)) == 0`.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Import of old SSLeay release: SSLeay 0.8.1b

+/* crypto/evp/evp_enc.c */
+/* Copyright (C) 1995-1997 Eric Young (eay@cryptsoft.com)
+ * All rights reserved.

+ * This package is an SSL implemen
bic_trace_step.reason: The function EVP_EncryptUpdate contains the vulnerable integer addition `if (i+inl < bl)`. If `inl` is extremely large, `i+inl` can overflow and appear to be less than `bl`, causing the subsequent `memcpy(&(ctx->buf[i]),in,inl);` to overflow the buffer.
